name: Tangible Real USD (USDR)

custodian.name: Tangible's UK SPV (real estate); Maker Vault (DAI)

auditor.name: Self-reported reserves; no Big Four audit

summary: Stablecoin backed by tokenized UK rental real estate (TNFT) + DAI + TNGBL share token. First major RWA-backed stablecoin failure: de-pegged October 11, 2023 when DAI liquid reserves (~14.74% of backing) were exhausted by redemptions. Real estate backing (~58%) was illiquid and could not be converted to honor withdrawals. Single most important case study for RWA-backed stablecoin liquidity mismatch.
underlying_asset: Tokenized UK rental properties (~58% at de-peg), DAI (~14.74%), TNGBL governance token (~13%), POL/MATIC liquidity (~14%). Real estate held via UK SPV; tenants paid rent in GBP, converted to crypto yield.
reserve_composition: Mixed: ~58% illiquid tokenized real estate (TNFT) + ~14.74% DAI (liquid) + ~13% TNGBL (reflexive) + remainder in POL liquidity. Liquid backing represented <30% of supply at de-peg.
secondary_market: Curve USDR-am3CRV pool on Polygon; thin liquidity post-incident.

smart_contract_audit.auditor: Unaudited at launch; later partial audits not publicly released

oracle_provider: Tangible-reported property NAV updated quarterly; no independent oracle. Asset valuations subject to issuer discretion.
redemption_mechanism: Direct redemption to DAI at 1:1 while DAI reserves available. Once DAI exhausted, redemption switched to TNFT real estate fractions — illiquid, non-USD denominated, with implicit haircut.
upgrade_authority: Tangible Labs multisig controlled protocol parameters and reserve composition.

timeline.event: TVL peaks at ~$45M; rental yield narrative drives growth
DAI reserves drop below 14.74%; large holder redemptions accelerate
DAI reserves exhausted; USDR de-pegs to $0.51 within hours
Tangible announces recovery plan; pivots to TNFT/locker distribution
USDR redemptions paused; recovery process begins
Recovery process ongoing; holders partially repaid in illiquid TNFTs

postmortem.root_cause: Asset-liability liquidity mismatch. USDR claimed daily redeemability against a reserve that was ~58% illiquid real estate. Only DAI portion (~14.74% at de-peg) could honor redemptions on-demand. When redemption demand exceeded DAI liquidity, the system had no mechanism to liquidate real estate quickly — UK property sales take months. Compounded by reflexive TNGBL component (~13%) that lost value as confidence dropped, and oracle dependency on issuer-reported property valuations with no independent verification.

what_failed.issue: Real estate is a legitimate asset class but inherently illiquid. Using it as backing for a daily-redeemable stablecoin created structural maturity mismatch. Additional reflexive TNGBL component (~13%) compounded the problem.
Redemption mechanism promised daily 1:1 to DAI while reserves were 58% illiquid. No tiered redemption schedule, no liquidity buffer minimum, no gate mechanism for orderly wind-down.
UK property SPV concentration; single-jurisdiction tenant base; rental yield denominated in GBP creating FX risk against USD peg. No diversified custody.
Property valuations self-reported by Tangible Labs without independent oracle or third-party appraiser. Quarterly updates created stale-data risk between reporting periods.

postmortem.outcome: USDR de-pegged to $0.51; ~$45M peak supply trapped. Holders partially repaid in illiquid TNFTs and locker tokens. The first significant RWA-backed stablecoin failure. Cited in HKMA stablecoin consultation paper (December 2023) and Bank of England discussion paper on systemic stablecoin requirements as evidence for liquid-reserve minimums.
postmortem.rarm_lesson: USDR is the canonical RWA stablecoin liquidity-mismatch case. RARM AFI for RWA-backed stablecoins must enforce a hard L4 constraint: liquid reserve fraction must equal or exceed maximum plausible daily redemption demand. For real estate backing, this typically means 30-50% liquid buffer or tiered redemption with explicit gates. Secondary lesson: L5 oracle requirements for RWA — issuer-reported NAV is not an oracle. RARM requires independent third-party valuation (Big Four, Knight Frank, or equivalent) at minimum quarterly with monthly attestation between.
